expectations.text: Delivers an actual policy document (purpose, scope, roles, policy statements, compliance) — not just a discussion of access control
expectations.evidence: Response includes 1. Purpose, 2. Scope, 3. Policy Statements (3.1-3.4), 4. Account Lifecycle Management, 5. Monitoring and Audit, 6. Roles and Responsibilities, 7. Exceptions, 8. Review — a full formal policy document.

expectations.text: Covers the four requested domains with NZISM-consistent requirements: unique identification of users, authentication strength (MFA for privileged and remote access), least-privilege authorisation, and privileged access management (separate admin accounts, logging, periodic review)
expectations.evidence: 3.1 requires unique identification and prohibits shared accounts; 3.2 requires MFA for 'Remote access... Access to systems processing RESTRICTED... All privileged/administrative accounts'; 3.3 requires least privilege; 3.4 Privileged Access Management requires accounts 'Separate from standard user accounts', 'Logged and monitored', 'Reviewed at a higher frequency (e.g. quarterly)'.

expectations.text: Cites NZISM structure credibly: references NZISM chapters/sections and control identifiers (CIDs) for access control and uses MUST/SHOULD compliance language consistent with the manual's convention
expectations.evidence: Response only uses vague parenthetical notes like '(Aligned to NZISM access control / identification and authentication chapter requirements.)' and '(Aligned to NZISM authentication controls...)' with no chapter/section numbers and no control identifiers (CIDs) anywhere in the document. No X.X.X.C.XX style citations are present, failing the specific CID citation requirement.

expectations.text: Includes joiner-mover-leaver / access review lifecycle requirements (timely revocation, periodic recertification of access rights)
expectations.evidence: Dedicated Section 4 'Account Lifecycle Management' covers Provisioning, Modification, De-provisioning ('Access must be revoked immediately upon termination... within a defined maximum timeframe... for role changes'), and Inactive accounts, plus 'Access rights must be reviewed at least annually... to ensure continued business need (access recertification)' in 3.3.

expectations.text: Includes policy governance: owner, review cycle, exceptions process requiring documented risk acceptance
expectations.evidence: Section 6 names CISO/ITSM as policy owner, Section 8 'Review... reviewed at least annually', Section 7 'Exceptions... must be documented, risk-assessed, and formally approved by the CISO, with compensating controls identified and a defined review/expiry date'.
